title: Review round 1: Migrate relay wire protocol to JSON-RPC 2.0
notes:
MAINTAINER RISK ASSESSMENT: 9 high-severity operational risks identified across relay auth, message routing, and client response handling.

CRITICAL RISKS (P1):
1. Error responses swallowed (.ok()) on BROADCAST, SET_NICKNAME, PUBLISH_KEY_PACKAGE, GET_KEY_PACKAGE, WHISPER — clients hang with zero error feedback (nie-hhmj.1)
2. Malformed JSON-RPC from relay silently dropped; no diagnostic context (nie-hhmj.2)
3. SET_NICKNAME store errors logged but no error response sent; client hangs (nie-hhmj.11)
4. register_user() fails but auth continues; user invisible in DirectoryList (nie-hhmj.16)
5. all_users() failures silently return empty vec; blank directory broadcast (nie-hhmj.17)

SERIOUS RISKS (P2):
6. subscription_expiry() DB errors silently treated as 'not subscribed' (nie-hhmj.3)
7. is_subscribed cached at connect time; never re-evaluated; Phase 3 bypass (nie-hhmj.8)
8. Whisper to offline recipients silently succeeds with no delivery confirmation (nie-hhmj.18)
9. broadcast() ignores send errors; blind delivery failures; no accountability (nie-hhmj.19)
10. No request-response correlation tracking; out-of-order responses assigned to wrong requests (nie-hhmj.6)
11. connection_manager never logs reconnection details; silent reconnection loops (nie-hhmj.10)

PATTERN: Silent failure dominates. Errors are logged but requests proceed or fail without client notification. Store failures in auth path crash to 'default' semantics (empty list, treat as unsubscribed). Broadcast side-effects (state mutations) are not validated for success.

SOLO MAINTAINER IMPACT: A maintainer debugging 'users can't form groups' or 'broadcast seems to vanish' has no diagnostic trail. Errors hide in INFO/WARN logs that look benign ('failed to deserialize notification') but mask protocol bugs or data corruption.

RECOMMENDATION: Before merging, implement:
1. Error responses MUST be sent on all store failures in request handlers
2. Store failures in critical paths (register_user, all_users) MUST fail-closed (disconnect client, don't continue)
3. Broadcast/Whisper delivery failures MUST be reported or collected for operator visibility
4. Add request-response tracking with timeout enforcement on client side
5. Add diagnostics to reconnection loop (attempt count, last error, backoff state)
